title: Delicate Buttery Indian Milk Solids Gulab Jamun
description: A traditional Indian dessert featuring crispy, buttery gulab jamun soaked in a sweet and fragrant syrup, made without onion or garlic for a friendly flavor profile.
instructions: Combine milk powder, flour, baking soda, and salt in a large mixing bowl.
Add melted butter to the dry ingredients and mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes pliable and soft.
Divide the dough into small balls, about 1 inch in diameter.
Heat oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and fry the dough balls until golden brown.
Remove the fried gulab jamun from the oil and soak them in gulab syrup for at least 30 minutes.
Combine rose water and lemon juice in a small bowl and add it to the gulab syrup.
Serve the gulab jamun warm or at room temperature, garnished with chopped nuts or pistachios if desired.
